lines.en_lit: he slaughtered them outside before the doors of the Sanctuary were opened he is exempt. What is the reason? Lacking opening is comparable to lacking an action.
And didn’t Rav Ḥisda say: slaughtered a Paschal lamb outside during the rest of the days of the year for its own sake, he is exempt, not for its own sake he is liable.
The reason not for its own sake. However, without specifying it is for its own sake and he is exempt. But why Let us say: Since it is fit within not for its own sake,
How can be compared? There, requires uprooting These do not require uprooting
Rabba bar Shimi taught these in Rabba, and he a difficulty between Rabba Rabba. And he resolved as we resolved

When Rav Dimi came he said Rabbi Yirmeya said Rabbi Yoḥanan slaughtered a Paschal lamb outside during the rest of the days of the year, whether for its own sake, or not for its own sake, he is exempt.
Rav Dimi said: I said this halakha before Rabbi Yirmeya Granted, for its own sake because it is not fit However, not for its own sake, why Isn’t it fit not for its own sake inside
And said to me Uprooting outside is not called uprooting.
When Ravin came he said Rabbi Yirmeya said Rabbi Yoḥanan slaughtered a Paschal lamb outside during the rest of the days of the year, whether for its own sake or not for its own sake, he is liable.
even for its own sake? Didn’t we learn lacking time? whether itself or due to owners.
And what is lacking time due to owners? A zav, a zava, a woman after childbirth, and a leper,
who sacrificed their sin-offering and, their guilt-offering outside are exempt. their burnt-offerings and peace-offerings outside they are liable.
And Rav Ḥilkiya bar Tovi said: They only taught for its own sake, but not for its own sake, he is liable,

for its own sake, in any event he is exempt. Why? Let us say: Since they are fit inside not for their own sake, How can be compared? There, requires uprooting Here, a Paschal lamb during the rest of the days of the year is a peace-offering,

Rav Ashi taught: He is liable, as we stated. Rav Yirmeya of Difti taught: He is exempt. held a Paschal lamb during the rest of the days of the year requires uprooting and uprooting outside is not called uprooting. And this disagrees with of Rav Ḥilkiya bar Tovi,
The Master said: after he drew lots them he is liable for for God, and exempt for to Azazel.

The Sages taught “Any man of the house of Israel who shall slaughter a bull, or a lamb, or a goat in the camp, or who slaughters it outside the camp, and has not brought it to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting to present it as an offering to the Lord
